steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: Wisconsin, ______ 1,372 feet in elevation, reaches its highest point at Timms Hill. The subject is Wisconsin and the main verb is reaches. The blank is set off by commas between the subject and main verb, so it must form a participial phrase that modifies Wisconsin — not a second finite verb, which would create a comma splice.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — spans: a finite present-tense verb — creates a comma splice with two independent clauses joined only by a comma. ✗
  • Choice B — spanning: a present participle — forms a participial phrase spanning 1,372 feet in elevation that correctly modifies Wisconsin without creating a second clause. ✓
  • Choice C — span: a finite verb (base form / third-person plural) — same comma-splice problem as A. ✗
  • Choice D — spanned: a past participle, but the action described is a current physical fact; more importantly, spanned would imply a passive or completed action, which does not fit the sentence's meaning. ✗
That matches Choice B — spanning.
